topic.what: Secure deployment of LLM agent pipelines on edge devices using hardware-backed confidential virtual machines
topic.how: Places agent runtime, inference engine, and third-party apps in independently attested Arm CCA realms with CSM communication channels
topic.why_matters: Protects proprietary assets (prompts, model weights) and sensitive runtime state (KV cache) from compromised OS/hypervisor on untrusted edge devices

characteristics.what_it_measures: Performance penalty compared to commodity OS multi-process deployment
characteristics.evidence: Table 1 chatbot end-to-end AgenTEE 98.25s vs NW Process 93.45s
characteristics.context: Prototype hardware (Radxa Rock 5B); results on commercial CCA expected better

characteristics.what_it_measures: Total time for agent-model query completion
characteristics.context: Chatbot agent with GPT2-Medium 437MB on OpenCCA prototype

characteristics.what_it_measures: LLM inference time within isolated engine
Performance cost relative to NW VM baseline
characteristics.evidence: Table 1 maximum end-to-end overhead vs NW VM across all settings
characteristics.context: AgenTEE with hardware isolation vs NW VM with trusted hypervisor

characteristics.what_it_measures: Lines of code in trusted computing base
characteristics.evidence: Section 2.3 RMM ~27K lines vs hypervisor ~300K lines
characteristics.context: Arm CCA reduces TCB by excluding hypervisor from security critical path

characteristics.what_itasures: Protection against normal-world access to sensitive state
characteristics.evidence: CCA realm memory protected from NW OS and hypervisor; KV cache and model weights inaccessible to privileged software
characteristics.context: Mutual distrust between agent provider, model provider, third-party apps

novelty: multi-realm agent architecture for confidential edge LLM agents
CSM-based inter-realm communication with mutual attestation
end-to-end confidential pipeline protecting KV cache state
practical evaluation on ARM CCA prototype
open_problems: commercial CCA hardware not yet available
no GPU/NPU acceleration support in current CCA
physical and microarchitectural side-channels out of scope
user must trust normal-world UI layer
